attractions.name: Banner Lakes at Summerset State Park
attractions.description: Banner Lakes at Summerset State Park is a 222-acre outdoor destination situated between Des Moines and Indianola on the site of a former 1930s-era coal mine. The park features two lakes formed from old mine pits, offering a unique rolling landscape for fishing, hiking, and mountain biking. Opened in 2004, it serves as a quiet retreat for visitors to explore diverse Iowa ecosystems including woodlands, prairies, and wetlands. (Iowa DNR, Wikipedia)
attractions.region: Warren County (between Des Moines and Indianola)
attractions.hours: Daily, sunrise to sunset (Warren County Conservation)
attractions.admission: Free (Iowa DNR, Experience Indianola)
highlights: "Six mountain bike singletrack trails with varying difficulty levels (e.g., Coal Miner's Daughter, Corner Pocket) ([Iowa DNR, Experience Indianola)", 'Two lakes stocked with trout, bluegill, channel catfish, crappie, and largemouth bass (Iowa DNR, Wikipedia)', 'A two-mile paved scenic bike loop that connects to the 12-mile Summerset Trail (Iowa DNR, Summerset Trail - Wikipedia)', 'The adjacent Banner Shooting Range, offering facilities for rifle, handgun, and archery practice (Iowa DNR)', 'Observation platform north of the lakes for birdwatching and scenic views (Warren County Conservation)']
attractions.tips: Visitors should check the Central Iowa Trail Association (CITA) website for trail closures during wet or muddy conditions to protect the terrain. It is recommended to bring water as drinking fountains may not be available year-round, and only primitive pit toilets are available on-site. (Central Iowa Trail Association, OnX Maps)
attractions.nearby: Banner Shooting Range, Summerset Trail, Annett Nature Center, Lake Ahquabi State Park, Indianola Balloon Classic (Iowa DNR, Experience Indianola, Warren County Conservation)

attractions.name: Beaverdale Neighborhood
attractions.description: Beaverdale is a historic and vibrant neighborhood in Des Moines, Iowa, renowned for its distinctive 'Beaverdale Brick' homes and tree-lined streets. As the largest of the city's 51 recognized neighborhoods, it offers a close-knit community atmosphere with numerous parks, local shops, and popular annual events like the Beaverdale Fall Festival.
attractions.address: P.O. Box 30175 (Mailing), Beaver Avenue and Urbandale Avenue (Business District)
attractions.hours: Open 24/7 (Neighborhood); Business and park hours vary.
attractions.admission: Free (Neighborhood access); Some events like the Fall Festival or Holiday Boutique may have fees.
highlights: ["Famous 'Beaverdale Brick' homes built primarily between 1920 and 1940.", 'Home to over 50 acres of public parkland, including Ashby, Beaverdale, and Witmer Parks.', 'Host of the annual Beaverdale Fall Festival, a major community celebration featuring a parade and carnival.', 'A walkable business district centered around Beaver and Urbandale Avenues with unique local shops and restaurants.', 'Strong community engagement through the Beaverdale Neighborhood Association (BNA).']
attractions.tips: Visit in September for the Fall Festival or in December to see the holiday light displays on Ashby and Wallace Avenues. The neighborhood is very walkable, making it easy to explore the local shops and unique brick architecture on foot.
attractions.nearby: VA Central Iowa Health Care System, Franklin Avenue Library, Waveland Golf Course, Tower Park.

attractions.name: Blank Park Zoo
attractions.description: Blank Park Zoo is a 49-acre zoological park in Des Moines, Iowa, and the only zoo in the state accredited by the Association of Zoos and Aquariums (AZA). The zoo's mission is to inspire an appreciation of the natural world through conservation, education, research, and recreation, featuring a diverse collection of over 100 species and 1,400 animals (Blank Park Zoo, Wikipedia).

attractions.admission: Children (1 year and under): Free; Children (2-12 years): $15.00; Adults (13-64 years): $20.00; Seniors (65+ years): $19.00 (Blank Park Zoo)
highlights: 'Indoor Discovery Center rainforest exhibit ([Blank Park Zoo)', 'Australia Adventure with wallabies and cassowaries (Wikipedia)', 'African Boardwalk featuring black rhinoceroses, giraffes, and zebras (Wikipedia)', 'Great Cats Complex and the upcoming Marjorie A. Foster Lion Conservation Center (Wikipedia)', 'Seal and Sea Lion Training Demonstrations (Blank Park Zoo)', 'Kids Kingdom with a petting zoo and play area (Wikipedia)']
attractions.tips: Advance tickets are encouraged but not required for entry. Guests are not allowed to bring outside food into the zoo, but re-entry is permitted with a hand stamp if you choose to picnic in nearby areas. The zoo is ADA accessible and offers sensory-friendly resources through its partnership with KultureCity (Blank Park Zoo).

attractions.name: Capital Square
attractions.description: Capital Square is a prominent eight-story Class A office and retail building located in the heart of downtown Des Moines. Known for its massive open atrium and 1980s Modernist architecture, the building serves as a central hub for the city's skywalk system and local commerce. (CBRE, Iowa Architectural Foundation)

highlights: 'Massive open atrium providing significant natural light throughout the interior ([LoopNet)', 'Designed by the world-renowned architecture firm Skidmore, Owings & Merrill (SOM) (Iowa Architectural Foundation)', 'Multiple integrated access points to the extensive Des Moines Skywalk System (CBRE)', 'Direct grade-level access to the adjacent Cowles Commons event plaza (CBRE)', 'Home to various retail services, including a U.S. Post Office and the Iowa Center for Architecture (Yelp, Iowa Architectural Foundation)', 'Historically significant as one of the first major urban renewal projects in downtown Des Moines (Business Record)']
attractions.tips: The atrium is a quiet, sun-drenched spot perfect for a break or a casual meeting, especially during the winter months. It also serves as a primary starting point for guided architectural walking tours of the city. (Yelp, Eventbrite)

Its main campus in Greenwood Park features three distinct buildings designed by renowned architects Eliel Saarinen, I.M. Pei, and Richard Meier, offering free exhibitions, art classes, and community programs (Official Website, Wikipedia).

attractions.description: Opened in June 1979, the Des Moines Civic Center is Iowa's largest theater and a premier venue for touring Broadway performances, concerts, and ballets. It features a fan-shaped continental seating area for over 2,700 guests and serves as the primary performance home for the Des Moines Symphony. (Des Moines Performing Arts, Wikipedia).

attractions.hours: Monday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM, Tuesday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM, Wednesday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM, Thursday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM, Friday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM, Saturday: Closed (Open 2 hours before shows), Sunday: Closed (Open 2 hours before shows). attractions.name: Hoyt Sherman Place
attractions.description: Hoyt Sherman Place is a premier historic cultural center in Des Moines, Iowa, encompassing a restored 1877 mansion, the city's first public art gallery, and a world-class 1,252-seat theater. It serves as a vibrant hub for concerts, comedy, and educational programs while preserving the legacy of Major Hoyt Sherman and the Des Moines Women’s Club. (Hoyt Sherman Place, DSM Partnership)

attractions.name: Insurance Exchange Building
attractions.description: The Insurance Exchange Building is a prominent 10-story historic office tower in downtown Des Moines, built in 1923 to house various insurance firms during the city's rise as a global insurance hub. Commissioned by George Peak, founder of the Central Life Assurance Society, the building features a unique irregular ell-shaped plan and a well-preserved Art Deco lobby. It is recognized for its Late 19th and Early 20th Century Revival architectural style and remains a key part of the city's commercial heritage.
attractions.hours: Monday: 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM, Tuesday: 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM, Wednesday: 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM, Thursday: 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM, Friday: 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM, Saturday: Closed, Sunday: Closed
attractions.admission: Free to view exterior; interiors typically restricted to tenants and business visitors.

attractions.tips: As a working office building, visitors are encouraged to respect the business environment when viewing the lobby. The exterior is best viewed from the corner of 5th Avenue and Grand Avenue to appreciate its irregular architecture. Parking is available in nearby downtown ramps or via metered street parking.

attractions.name: Iowa Hall of Pride (IHSAA Achieve)
attractions.description: The Iowa Hall of Pride was a 26,000-square-foot interactive museum that celebrated the achievements of Iowa high school athletes, performers, and notable citizens across sports, arts, and science. It featured high-tech displays, a Hall of Heroes, and hands-on activities designed to inspire youth and honor Iowa's heritage (Iowa History Journal). While the physical location closed in June 2022, its legacy lives on through the online "Achieve" program run by the Iowa High School Athletic Association (Iowa Events Center).

attractions.name: Register's Annual Great Bicycle Ride Across Iowa (RAGBRAI)
attractions.description: The Register’s Annual Great Bicycle Ride Across Iowa (RAGBRAI) is the oldest, largest, and longest recreational bicycle touring event in the world. This annual seven-day celebration of Iowa attracts riders from all 50 states and numerous foreign countries to pedal across the state from the Missouri River to the Mississippi River (RAGBRAI, Wikipedia).

attractions.region: Downtown Des Moines / East Village (Des Moines Register, Catch Des Moines)
attractions.hours: Route Support: 6:00 AM – 6:00 PM daily; Baggage Collection: 5:00 AM – 8:00 AM daily; Overnight Town Service Times: vary by town, typically ending between 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM on a rolling basis (RAGBRAI, RAGBRAI 101)
attractions.days_open: Saturday - Saturday (7-day event plus Day Zero Expo) (RAGBRAI)
attractions.admission: Registration Fees (2026 Tiers): Early (Nov-Dec): $230 Weeklong Rider, $45 Weeklong Non-Rider/Day Pass; Tier 1 (Jan-Feb): $250 Rider; Tier 2 (Mar): $275 Rider; Tier 3 (Apr): $300 Rider; Tier 4 (May): $350 Rider; Late/On-site (June-July): $400 Rider (Hawkeye Bicycle Association, RAGBRAI)
highlights: "Annual 'Tire Dip' ceremony in the Missouri River at the start and the Mississippi River at the finish ([RAGBRAI)", 'Overnight stays in host communities featuring nightly live music, local food vendors, and entertainment (RAGBRAI)', 'The Day Zero Expo held the Saturday before the ride starts, showcasing the latest cycling gear and accessories (RAGBRAI)', 'Support services including the Iowa State Patrol traffic control, roving medical support, and SAG vehicle assistance (RAGBRAI)', "Iconic roadside treats like 'Mr. Pork Chop' and local homemade pies along the daily routes (RAGBRAI)", 'Interactive RAGBRAI Founder’s Statue honoring John Karras and Don Kaul at Water Works Park in Des Moines (RAGBRAI)']
attractions.tips: Register early to avoid price increases and ensure registration materials are mailed to you before the ride. Be prepared for Iowa's summer weather (hills and heat), and take advantage of the baggage transport service by ensuring your bags are at the truck before the 8:00 AM sharp departure (RAGBRAI, RAGBRAI 101).
